title: Abdollah Khan appointed governor of Khuzestan; titles granted to sons
description: The shah appointed Abdollah Khan governor of Khuzestan and titled him Sardar Akram. At his request Hossein Qoli Khan received Sa'ed al-Saltaneh and Mohtaj Ali Khan received Ejlal al-Mamalek.

title: Gharagozloo participation in Mohammad Shah’s Herat campaign
description: Thousands of Gharagozloo troops served under Nabi Khan. Abdollah Khan supported his force; Gharagozloo troops helped assault the fortifications. Nabi Khan was killed after severe wounds.

title: British occupation of Bushehr and dispersal of the Gharagozloo guard
description: British forces attacked Bushehr to pressure Iran to withdraw from Herat. The fifty-man Gharagozloo guard was driven off because of its small size.

title: Gharagozloo participation in the Anglo-Persian War in southern Iran
description: Mostafa Qoli Khan, Rustam Khan, and Ali Khan served against British forces around Bandar Lengeh, Bushehr, Dalaki, and Borazjan.

title: Defeat of Nasrallah Khan of Laristan
description: Ali Naqi Khan led the Dargazin regiment and defeated Nasrallah Khan in Bikhuh district.

title: Gharagozloo military service in Kerman
description: Ali Khan Nosrat al-Molk and other Gharagozloo officers served in Kerman; Mohammad Hossein Khan later received the title Hosam al-Molk.

title: Marriage of Gholamreza Khan and Qamar al-Saltaneh
description: A lavish dynastic marriage arranged by Zeyn al-Abedin Khan Hosam al-Molk.

title: Arrest of Hamadan Gharagozloo amir-tumans
description: Following unrest in Hamadan, Hosam al-Molk, Saed al-Saltaneh, and Zia al-Molk were dismissed and detained.

title: Hosam al-Molk appointed governor of Kermanshah
description: After extensive attempts to obtain provincial office, Zeyn al-Abedin Khan Hosam al-Molk was appointed governor of Kermanshah.

title: Aqa Jan Gharagozloo and the Russian embassy case
description: After Pavlov was killed in an altercation involving Aqa Jan’s servant, Aqa Jan fled, was arrested in Hamadan, imprisoned in Tehran, and handed to the Russian embassy.

title: Gharagozloo regiments sent against unrest in Shiraz
description: Gharagozloo regiments under Saed al-Saltaneh were sent to suppress unrest in Shiraz; seven or eight people were killed.

title: Mahmoud Khan appointed colonel and successor to Nabi Khan’s command
description: Mohammad Shah appointed Mahmoud Khan colonel and placed Nabi Khan’s former troops under his command.

title: Mahmoud Khan mission to Russia
description: Mahmoud Khan served as Iranian envoy to Russia and returned with promotion and royal honors.

title: Mahmoud Khan steam spinning factory project
description: Mahmoud Khan imported machinery from Moscow for a modern steam-powered spinning factory; the project failed for lack of trained operators.

title: Mahmoud Khan appointed first minister of commerce
description: Mahmoud Khan Naser al-Molk became the first holder of the newly established ministry of commerce.

title: Deaths of major Gharagozloo khans
description: The book records the deaths of Abdollah Khan Sarem al-Saltaneh, Ali Naqi Khan, Ali Khan Nosrat al-Molk, Amanollah Khan, Mostafa Qoli Khan, Mahmoud Khan, and Mohammad Hossein Khan Hosam al-Molk.

title: Abdollah Khan appointed governor of Khuzestan
description: Abdollah Khan Saed al-Saltaneh was appointed governor and received the title Sardar Akram.

title: Foundation of the Islamiyyeh School at Shervin
description: Hosam al-Molk and Zia al-Molk established the Islamiyyeh School.

title: Gharagozloo grain blockade and Hamadan bread crisis
description: Gharagozloo landowners restricted grain deliveries during conflict with the Majles-e Favaed-e Ammeh and Constitutionalists.

title: Killing of Seyyed Jamal al-Din Vaez Esfahani
description: The preacher was delivered to Amir Afkham’s circle and killed in Borujerd.

title: Gharagozloo regiments against Tabriz Constitutionalists
description: Regiments commanded by Sardar Akram, Mansur al-Dowleh, and Amir Afkham supported Mohammad Ali Shah against Tabriz.

title: Gharagozloo participation in the Salar al-Dowleh crisis
description: Amir Nezam and other khans shifted positions during Salar al-Dowleh’s rebellion; Amir Afkham later joined government forces.

title: Naser al-Molk dissolves the Second Majles
description: As regent, Naser al-Molk ordered Yeprem Khan to close the Second Majles during the Russian ultimatum crisis.

title: Russian occupation of Hamadan and appointment of Amir Afkham
description: Russian forces occupied Hamadan with assistance from Amir Afkham, who was installed as governor.

title: Arrest and release of Amir Nezam after the 1921 coup
description: Amir Nezam was arrested by Seyyed Zia’s government and reportedly paid 25,000 tomans to secure release.

title: Gharagozloo participation in the 1304 SH Constituent Assembly
description: Zia al-Molk Farmand represented Hamadan in the assembly that confirmed Reza Shah.
